title: Analyzing transposable element expression with TExpress
text: TExpress R package downstream analysis transposable element (TE) expression quantification tools TElocal (part TEtranscripts suite). addition performing differential expression analysis DESeq2, TExpress annotates TEs respect genomic features classifies expression originating either TE’s promoter (self-expressed) external promoter, typically nearby gene (gene-dependent). analysis organized three-stage pipeline, stage consumes extends single result list: TE_DEA() — reads metadata count tables, runs DESeq2, returns list res.TEs, TE.count, gene.count, metadata. annotate_TE_regions() — adds genomic-context annotation (Promoter / Exon / Intron / …) relative gene GTF. classify_TE_transcription() — labels TE self-expressed gene-dependent. stage also writes publication-ready figures result tables disk. figures shown throughout vignette generated running pipeline example dataset described . TExpress pipeline overview: output stage input next.
code: library(TExpress)

title: Analyzing transposable element expression with TExpress
text: TE features identified colon-separated, four-level hierarchy used row name every TE table: presence : feature’s row name TExpress distinguishes TEs genes throughout package, format must preserved.
code: TE_element : TE_name : TE_family : TE_class e.g.  L1PA2_dup501 : L1PA2 : L1 : LINE

title: Analyzing transposable element expression with TExpress
text: metadata table must contain Condition column exactly values "Control" "Treat" (case-sensitive). TExpress uses Control reference level, every reported contrast Treat vs. Control. minimal metadata file tab-separated table four columns: File, Sample, Group, Condition.

title: Analyzing transposable element expression with TExpress
text: chunks evaluated vignette built (require Bioconductor stack ~90 MB download), show complete, runnable analysis. figures accompanying step real outputs produced exact calls example chr22 dataset.

title: Analyzing transposable element expression with TExpress
text: downloadTestData() fetches small chr22 example dataset (gene GTF, TE GTF, count tables) returns paths needed pipeline.
code: my.data <- downloadTestData()  str(my.data) #> List of 4 #>  $ metafile     : chr ".../data.csv" #>  $ folder       : chr "..." #>  $ gtf.gene.file: chr ".../Homo_sapiens.GRCh38.115_chr22.gtf" #>  $ gtf.TE.file  : chr ".../hg38_rmsk_TE_chr22.gtf"

title: Analyzing transposable element expression with TExpress
text: TE_DEA() reads metadata counts, imports TE GTF, runs DESeq2, writing volcano/MA plots result tables output. returned object list carrying result data frames counts following stages extend. TE results, TE_DEA() writes volcano plot MA plot (matching pair also produced genes): Step 1 — differential expression TEs: volcano plot (left) MA plot (right). - -regulated TEs highlighted.
code: TE_results <- TE_DEA(   metafile    = my.data$metafile,   folder      = my.data$folder,   gtf.TE.file = my.data$gtf.TE.file,   output      = "results",   maxpadj     = 0.05,   minlfc      = 1,   device      = c("png", "svg"),   plot.title  = "DGCR8-KO vs WT" )

title: Analyzing transposable element expression with TExpress
text: annotate_TE_regions() assigns TE genomic region (Promoter, 5’ UTR, Exon, Intron, 3’ UTR, Downstream, Intergenic) relative supplied gene annotation, produces stacked bar plots region TE-class distributions. stacked bar plots compare, side side, genomic-region TE-class composition TEs expressed condition /-regulated sets: Step 2 — distribution TEs across genomic regions (left) TE classes (right) control, treatment, - -regulated groups.
code: TE_results <- annotate_TE_regions(   TE_results    = TE_results,   gtf.genes.file = my.data$gtf.gene.file,   output_folder = "results",   device        = c("png", "svg"),   minCounts     = 10,   plot.title = "DGCR8-KO vs WT" )

title: Analyzing transposable element expression with TExpress
text: Finally, classify_TE_transcription() labels expressed TEs self-expressed (transcribed internal promoter) gene-dependent (transcribed part host-gene transcription). save argument selects set TEs analyze: "", "", "", "dys" (+ ). Among others, step produces bar plot self-expressed fraction per TE class bar plot TE families contributing self-expressed loci: Step 3 — self-expressed TEs broken TE class (left) families contributing self-expressed loci (right).
code: TE_results <- classify_TE_transcription(   TE_results    = TE_results,   output_folder = "results",   plot.title    = "DGCR8-KO vs WT",   save          = "dys" )

title: Analyzing transposable element expression with TExpress
text: Several plotting functions can called directly result data frame, outside three-stage pipeline. useful focusing particular set TEs re-plotting different thresholds. operate TE_results$res.TEs (TE results data frame, carries log2FoldChange, padj, TE_element / TE_name / TE_family / TE_class columns).

title: Analyzing transposable element expression with TExpress
text: violinPlotByTEList() draws one violin per element TE list sit level hierarchy — example four main TE classes — showing distribution log2 fold changes, significantly - -regulated loci overlaid points: violinPlotByTEList(): log2 fold-change distribution TE class. violinPlotByTEtype() zooms single TE type shows dysregulated members finer level (, top LINE families name): violinPlotByTEtype(): dysregulated LINE elements, one violin per name.
code: violinPlotByTEList(   res.TEs    = TE_results$res.TEs,   TE_list    = c("LINE", "SINE", "LTR", "DNA"),   minlfc     = 1,   maxpadj    = 0.05,   plot.title = "TE expression by class",   output_folder = "results/TEs_DEA" ) violinPlotByTEtype(   res.TEs       = TE_results$res.TEs,   TE_type       = "LINE",   specific_type = "TE_name",   nTop          = 6,   plot.title    = "Most dysregulated LINE families",   output_folder = "results/TEs_DEA" )

what: Re-using the pipeline plot functions directly
title: Analyzing transposable element expression with TExpress
text: figures shown standard workflow produced internally exported functions can also call : graphTE_DEA() (volcano + MA), graphTEregion() (region / TE-class stacked bars) graph_classify_TE() (classification bar plots). Calling directly lets re-plot results different significance thresholds, output formats titles. instance, redraw TE volcano/MA pair stricter cutoffs: graphTE_DEA() called directly stricter thresholds (padj < 0.01, |log2FC| > 1.5). functions can saved several formats via device argument (e.g. device = c("png", "svg")), low-level helper printDevice() exported saving arbitrary ggplot object multiple formats.
code: graphTE_DEA(   res           = TE_results$res.TEs,   maxpadj       = 0.01,   minlfc        = 1.5,   device        = "png",   output_folder = "results/custom",   plot.title    = "TEs (custom thresholds)" )

title: Analyzing transposable element expression with TExpress
text: per-class donut chart expression-type breakdown, create_pie_donut() also available. depends webr package, longer maintained emits deprecation warnings, run vignette; see ?create_pie_donut usage.

title: Analyzing transposable element expression with TExpress
text: aware reads overlapping gene feature annotation TE annotation strand assigned genes TElocal. interested restricting gene annotation features (example, protein_coding lncRNA) can use TExpress function filter_GTF:
code: filter_GTF(   "Homo_sapiens.GRCh38.115.gtf",   features   = c("protein_coding"),   format.in  = "gtf",   format.out = "gtf",   suffix     = "filtered" )

title: Analyzing transposable element expression with TExpress

what: Tools for Analyzing Transposable Element (TE) Expression
title: Tools for Analyzing Transposable Element (TE) Expression
text: Comprehensive tools analyzing transposable element (TE) expression, genomic annotation, transcriptional classification. TExpress provides complete workflow differential expression analysis transposable elements, including: Statistical analysis using DESeq2 Genomic context annotation relative protein-coding genes Visualization TE expression patterns Classification TEs transcriptional origin (self-expressed vs. gene-dependent) Transposable Elements Development Disease - Sara R. Heras lab

title: Tools for Analyzing Transposable Element (TE) Expression
text: TExpress organized three-stage pipeline. function consumes extends single TE_results object, output one stage input next: TE_DEA() runs differential expression analysis, annotate_TE_regions() adds genomic-context annotation, classify_TE_transcription() labels TE self-expressed gene-dependent. detailed diagram showing functions called inside stage available man/figures/pipeline.svg.

title: Tools for Analyzing Transposable Element (TE) Expression
text: Install development version GitHub: Please, notice issue gghalves packages can solved following link.
code: # Install remotes if needed if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = TRUE)) {   install.packages("remotes") }  # Install TExpress remotes::install_github("GuillePeris/TExpress")

title: Tools for Analyzing Transposable Element (TE) Expression
code: library(TExpress)  # Download test data my.data <- downloadTestData()  # Run differential expression analysis TE_results_DEA <- TE_DEA(   metafile = my.data$metafile,   folder = my.data$folder,   gtf.TE.file = my.data$gtf.TE.file,   output = "results",   plot.title = "My Analysis" )  # Annotate TEs with genomic context TE_results_annot <- annotate_TE_regions(   TE_results = TE_results_DEA,   gtf.genes.file = my.data$gtf.gene.file,   output_folder = "results" )  # Classify TEs as self-expressed or gene-dependent TE_results_classify <- classify_TE_transcription(TE_results_annot,    output_folder = "results"),

title: Tools for Analyzing Transposable Element (TE) Expression
text: TExpress designed work count tables generated TElocal TEtranscripts package. Follow TElocal reccomendations run software. Please, notice download pre-built TE annotation run TElocal. want use TE annotation, follow instructions . Also, aware reads overlapping gene feature annotation TE annotation strand assigned genes TElocal. interested restricting gene annotation features (example, protein_coding lncRNA) can use TExpress function filter_GTF:
code: gtf.genes.file <- "Homo_sapiens.GRCh38.115.gtf" filter_GTF(gtf.genes.file,             features = c("protein_coding"),            format.in = "gtf",            format.out = "gtf",            suffix = "filtered")

title: Tools for Analyzing Transposable Element (TE) Expression
text: tab-separated text file describing samples. file must contain exactly four columns specific headers requirements: Example metadata file: ⚠️ Important: Condition column must contain exactly “Control” “Treat” (case-sensitive) DESeq2 analysis.
code: File    Sample  Group   Condition TElocal_WT1.cntTable    WT1 WT  Control TElocal_WT2.cntTable    WT2 WT  Control TElocal_WT3.cntTable    WT3 WT  Control TElocal_WT4.cntTable    WT4 WT  Control TElocal_KO1.cntTable    KO1 KO  Treat TElocal_KO2.cntTable    KO2 KO  Treat TElocal_KO3.cntTable    KO3 KO  Treat TElocal_KO4.cntTable    KO4 KO  Treat

title: Tools for Analyzing Transposable Element (TE) Expression
text: TE GTF file: TE annotation matching one used TElocal. Gene GTF file: Gene annotation file. must used TElocal extended version including features TElocal gene annotation.

title: Tools for Analyzing Transposable Element (TE) Expression
text: Perform statistical analysis identify differentially expressed TEs conditions. Output: DESeq2 results genes TEs (separate directories) Normalized count matrices Volcano plots MA plots Summary statistics
code: library(TExpress)  # Download test data (or specify your own paths) my.data <- downloadTestData()  # Define parameters metafile <- my.data$metafile        # Path to metadata file folder <- my.data$folder            # Directory with count files gtf.TE.file <- my.data$gtf.TE.file  # TE annotation GTF output <- "results"                 # Output directory maxpadj <- 0.05                     # Adjusted p-value threshold minlfc <- 1                         # Log2 fold change threshold (2-fold) device <- c("png", "svg")           # Output formats for plots plot.title <- "DGCR8-KO vs WT"      # Title for plots  # Run differential expression analysis TE_results <- TE_DEA(   metafile = metafile,   folder = folder,   output = output,   maxpadj = maxpadj,   minlfc = minlfc,   gtf.TE.file = gtf.TE.file,   device = device,   plot.title = plot.title )

title: Tools for Analyzing Transposable Element (TE) Expression
text: TExpress uses hierarchical classification system TEs: Example: L1PA2_dup501:L1PA2:L1:LINE TE_class: Broad category (LINE, SINE, LTR, DNA) TE_family: Family within class (L1, Alu, ERVK, etc.) TE_name: Specific TE type (L1PA2, AluY, etc.) TE_element: Individual genomic locus (L1PA2_dup501) hierarchy allows flexible visualization different taxonomic levels.
code: TE_element : TE_name : TE_family : TE_class

title: Tools for Analyzing Transposable Element (TE) Expression
text: Annotate TEs genomic location relative protein-coding genes. Output: Annotated TE results genomic coordinates Gene associations (nearest gene TE) Distribution across genomic regions (Promoter, Exon, Intron, etc.) Distribution across TE classes (LINE, SINE, LTR, DNA) Genomic Regions Annotated: Promoter: Within 1000 bp (default) upstream/downstream TSS 5’ UTR: Five-prime untranslated region Exon: Protein-coding exons Intron: Intronic regions 3’ UTR: Three-prime untranslated region Downstream: 10kb (default) downstream gene end Intergenic: associated gene feature
code: TE_results <- annotate_TE_regions(   TE_results = TE_results,   gtf.genes.file = my.data$gtf.gene.file,   output_folder = output,   device = c("png", "svg"),   plot.title = plot.title,   minCounts = 10  # Minimum normalized counts for inclusion )

title: Tools for Analyzing Transposable Element (TE) Expression
text: Classify TEs : Self-expressed: Transcribed internal promoter Gene-dependent: Transcribed part host gene transcription (runthrough) Use save variable analyze “” expressed TEs, “”-regulated, “”-regulated “dys”-regulated (+).
code: TE_results_dys <- classify_TE_transcription(TE_results,                                              output_folder = output,                                              plot.title = plot.title,                                             save = "dys")

title: Tools for Analyzing Transposable Element (TE) Expression
text: package includes test dataset chromosome 22: Dataset details: Comparison: DGCR8-KO vs. WT Organism: Human (hg38) Chromosome: chr22 (quick testing) Replicates: 4 per condition Reference: GSE197472
code: # Download to temporary directory my.data <- downloadTestData()  # Or specify custom location my.data <- downloadTestData(folder = "~/data/TExpress_test")

title: Tools for Analyzing Transposable Element (TE) Expression
text: use TExpress research, please cite zenodo DOI.  Peris, Guillermo. TExpress: Tools analyzing transposable element expression (2026). https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20382521.

what: Perform Differential Expression Analysis for Transposable Elements — TE_DEA
title: Perform Differential Expression Analysis for Transposable Elements — TE_DEA
text: Main pipeline function analyzing differential expression transposable elements (TEs) genes using DESeq2. Reads count data, performs statistical analysis, generates visualization plots.

title: Perform Differential Expression Analysis for Transposable Elements — TE_DEA
code: TE_DEA(   metafile,   folder,   output = ".",   maxpadj = 0.05,   minlfc = 1,   gtf.TE.file,   device = "png",   plot.title = "",   useCtrlGenes = FALSE,   shrinklog2FC = FALSE,   saveNorm = TRUE )

title: Perform Differential Expression Analysis for Transposable Elements — TE_DEA
text: metafile Character string. Full path metadata file. tab-separated file readable read_metadata. folder Character string. Full path directory containing count files listed metadata. output Character string. Path output directory. created exist. Default current directory ("."). maxpadj Numeric. Adjusted p-value threshold significance. Features padj < maxpadj considered significant. Default 0.05. minlfc Numeric. Minimum absolute log2 fold change threshold differential expression. Default 1. gtf.TE.file Character string. Full path GTF file containing TE annotations. Must GTF file used upstream TE counting TElocal TEtranscript package. device Character vector. File format(s) output plots. Supported: "svg", "eps", "png", "tiff", "jpeg". Can specify multiple formats vector (e.g., c("jpeg", "png")). Default "png". plot.title Character string. Title volcano MA plots. empty string (default), generic titles used. useCtrlGenes Logical. TRUE, uses genes (TEs) estimating DESeq2 size factors. can improve normalization TEs different expression patterns genes. Default FALSE. shrinklog2FC Logical. TRUE, applies apeglm log2 fold change shrinkage DESeq2 accurate effect size estimates. Recommended ranking visualization. Default FALSE. saveNorm Logical. TRUE, saves normalized count matrices genes TEs. FALSE, saves DESeq2 results. Default TRUE.

title: Perform Differential Expression Analysis for Transposable Elements — TE_DEA
text: list four elements: res.TEs Data frame DESeq2 results TEs, including genomic coordinates TE annotations TE.count Data frame normalized counts TEs across samples gene.count Data frame normalized counts genes across samples metadata Original metadata data frame used analysis

title: Perform Differential Expression Analysis for Transposable Elements — TE_DEA
text: pipeline performs following steps: Reads sample metadata count files Imports TE annotations GTF file Adds genomic coordinates TE features Filters non-standard chromosomes Performs DESeq2 differential expression analysis Extracts normalized counts Separates results genes vs. TEs Saves results separate directories Generates volcano MA plots genes TEs function distinguishes TEs genes presence colon (:) feature name, standard format TElocal TE loci identifiers: TE_element:TE_name:TE_family:TE_class (e.g., "L1PA2_dup501:L1PA2:L1:LINE"). Gene identifiers contain colon (e.g., "ENSMUSG00000000001").

title: Perform Differential Expression Analysis for Transposable Elements — TE_DEA
text: function creates following directory structure: TE results include additional columns genomic coordinates (seqnames, start, end, strand, width) TE class/family information.
code: output/   ├── genes_DEA/   │   ├── DESeq2_gene_results.tsv   │   ├── gene_normalizedCounts.tsv (if saveNorm = TRUE)   │   ├── volcanoPlot.<format>   │   └── maPlot.<format>   └── TEs_DEA/       ├── DESeq2_TE_results.tsv       ├── TE_normalizedCounts.tsv (if saveNorm = TRUE)       ├── volcanoPlot.<format>       └── maPlot.<format>

title: Perform Differential Expression Analysis for Transposable Elements — TE_DEA
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic usage with default parameters results <- TE_DEA(   metafile = "metadata.txt",   folder = "counts",   gtf.TE.file = "TEs.gtf" )  # Full analysis with custom parameters results <- TE_DEA(   metafile = "samples.txt",   folder = "count_data",   output = "results/DE_analysis",   maxpadj = 0.01,   minlfc = 1.5,   gtf.TE.file = "annotations/TE_annotation.gtf",   device = c("tiff", "png"),   plot.title = "KO vs WT",   useCtrlGenes = TRUE,   shrinklog2FC = TRUE,   saveNorm = TRUE )  # Access TE results head(results$res.TEs) dim(results$TE.count)  # Find significantly upregulated TEs sig_up_TEs <- results$res.TEs[   results$res.TEs$padj < 0.05 &   results$res.TEs$log2FoldChange > 1, ] } # }

what: Add Genomic Coordinates to TE Count Data — TE_coords
title: Add Genomic Coordinates to TE Count Data — TE_coords
text: Extracts transposable element (TE) information count data rownames adds genomic coordinates joining TE annotation data.

title: Add Genomic Coordinates to TE Count Data — TE_coords
code: TE_coords(countData.TEs, TE_annot.df)

title: Add Genomic Coordinates to TE Count Data — TE_coords
text: countData.TEs Data frame TE count data. Rownames must follow format: "TE_element:TE_name:TE_family:TE_class" (colon-separated). column contain count data sample. TE_annot.df Data frame TE annotations, typically derived GTF file. Must contain columns: seqnames, start, end, strand, transcript_id. transcript_id column used match first field countData.TEs rownames.

title: Add Genomic Coordinates to TE Count Data — TE_coords
text: Data frame containing original count data plus additional columns: TE_element: Transcript ID extracted rownames TE_name: TE name extracted rownames TE_family: TE family extracted rownames TE_class: TE class extracted rownames seqnames: Chromosome/sequence name annotation start: Start position annotation end: End position annotation strand: Strand information annotation Original rownames preserved. @importFrom rlang .data

title: Add Genomic Coordinates to TE Count Data — TE_coords
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Assuming countData.TEs has rownames like: # "L1PA2_dup501:L1PA2:L1:LINE" # "AluY_dup68349:AluY:Alu:SINE"  # And TE_annot.df has columns: transcript_id, seqnames, start, end, strand  countData_with_coords <- TE_coords(countData.TEs, TE_annot.df)  # Check results head(countData_with_coords) } # }

what: Annotate TEs with Genomic Context Relative to Genes — TE_genomic_context
title: Annotate TEs with Genomic Context Relative to Genes — TE_genomic_context
text: Annotates transposable element (TE) loci genomic context relative protein-coding genes. Uses ChIPseeker determine TEs overlap promoters, exons, introns, UTRs, intergenic regions. Assigns nearest gene intergenic TEs.

title: Annotate TEs with Genomic Context Relative to Genes — TE_genomic_context
code: TE_genomic_context(   df.TEs,   gene.TxDb,   gene_names,   transcript.gr,   TSSminus,   TSSplus,   downstream,   is_ext_3UTR = FALSE,   ext_3UTR_file = NULL )

title: Annotate TEs with Genomic Context Relative to Genes — TE_genomic_context
text: df.TEs Data frame containing TE loci genomic coordinates. Must include columns: seqnames, start, end, strand. Typically output differential expression analysis. gene.TxDb TxDb object containing gene/transcript annotations. Can created GTF files using GenomicFeatures::makeTxDbFromGFF loaded Bioconductor annotation packages. gene_names Data frame mapping gene IDs gene names. Must contain columns: gene_id (matching IDs TxDb) gene_name (human-readable gene symbols). Used add gene names final output. transcript.gr GRanges object containing transcript annotations. Must include gene_id column metadata. Used assigning nearest gene intergenic TEs. Typically obtained filtering gene GTF entries type == "transcript". TSSminus Integer. Number bases upstream TSS define promoter region. negative. Default -1000 (1kb upstream). TSSplus Integer. Number bases downstream TSS define promoter region. positive. Default 1000 (1kb downstream). downstream Integer. Maximum distance downstream gene end consider "Downstream" annotation. Default 10000 (10kb). is_ext_3UTR Boolean. TRUE extended 3'UTR analysis performed. Defaults FALSE. implemented ext_3UTR_file Character string. Filename 3'UTR analysis result, gff format. implemented.

title: Annotate TEs with Genomic Context Relative to Genes — TE_genomic_context
text: Data frame combining original TE information genomic annotation columns: annotation Simplified genomic context (Promoter, 5' UTR, Exon, Intron, 3' UTR, Downstream, Intergenic) geneChr, geneStart, geneEnd, geneLength, geneStrand Genomic coordinates associated gene geneId Gene identifier TxDb gene_name Human-readable gene symbol (available gene_names) distanceToTSS Distance transcription start site (ChIPseeker)

title: Annotate TEs with Genomic Context Relative to Genes — TE_genomic_context
text: function performs genomic annotation several steps: Converts TE data frame GRanges object Annotates TEs using ChIPseeker priority coding regions Simplifies annotation categories (e.g., "Exon (uc057wby.1/267097, exon 2 7)" becomes "Exon") intergenic TEs, assigns nearest transcript transcript.gr Adds gene names provided mapping table Annotation priorities (highest lowest): 5' UTR → 3' UTR → Exon → Promoter → Intron → Downstream → Intergenic Note: ChIPseeker may internally convert UCSC chromosome naming (chr1) NCBI style (1). function converts back UCSC format consistency.

title: Annotate TEs with Genomic Context Relative to Genes — TE_genomic_context
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Annotate TEs (assuming TE_results from TE_DEA) TE_annotated <- TE_genomic_context(   df.TEs = TE_results$res.TEs,   gene.TxDb = txdb,   gene_names = gene_names,   transcript.gr = transcript.gr,   TSSminus = -3000,   TSSplus = 3000 ) } # }

what: Annotate TEs with Genomic Regions and Generate Region Distribution Plots — annotate_TE_regions
title: Annotate TEs with Genomic Regions and Generate Region Distribution Plots — annotate_TE_regions
text: Annotates transposable elements (TEs) genomic context relative protein-coding genes generates stacked bar plots showing distribution TEs across genomic regions (promoters, exons, introns, etc.). wrapper function combines genomic annotation visualization.

title: Annotate TEs with Genomic Regions and Generate Region Distribution Plots — annotate_TE_regions
code: annotate_TE_regions(   TE_results,   gtf.genes.file,   gtf.format = "gtf",   output_folder = ".",   device = "png",   gene_biotypes = "protein_coding",   plot.title = NULL,   maxpadj = 0.05,   minlfc = 1,   width = 10,   height = 7,   minCounts = 10,   TSSminus = -1000,   TSSplus = 1000,   downstream = 10000,   is_ext_3UTR = FALSE,   ext_3UTR_file = NULL )

title: Annotate TEs with Genomic Regions and Generate Region Distribution Plots — annotate_TE_regions
text: TE_results List object returned TE_DEA, containing: res.TEs Data frame DESeq2 results TEs TE.count Data frame normalized counts TEs gene.count Data frame normalized counts genes metadata Sample metadata data frame gtf.genes.file Character string. Full path GTF file containing gene annotations. include protein-coding genes transcript information. Must columns: type, transcript_biotype, gene_biotype, gene_id, gene_name. gtf.format Character string. Format gene GTF file: "gtf" (default), "gff3· "gff". output_folder Character string. Path output directory. subdirectory "TEs_annotated" created inside folder. Default current directory ("."). device Character vector. File format(s) output plots. Supported: "svg", "eps", "png", "tiff", "jpeg". Can specify multiple formats. Default "png". gene_biotypes Character vector. Features exons. Default "protein_coding", plot.title Character string. Title region distribution plots. NULL (default), generic title used. maxpadj Numeric. Adjusted p-value threshold significance. Features padj < maxpadj considered significant. Default 0.05. minlfc Numeric. Minimum absolute log2 fold change threshold differential expression. Default 1. width Numeric. Plot width inches. Default 10. height Numeric. Plot height inches. Default 7. minCounts Numeric. Minimum total normalized counts threshold considering TE loci expressed. Used region distribution plots. Default 10. TSSminus Integer. Number bases upstream TSS define promoter region. negative. Default -1000 (1kb upstream). TSSplus Integer. Number bases downstream TSS define promoter region. positive. Default 1000 (1kb downstream). downstream Integer. Maximum distance downstream gene end consider "Downstream" annotation. Default 10000 (10kb). is_ext_3UTR Boolean. TRUE extended 3'UTR analysis performed. Defaults FALSE. implemented ext_3UTR_file Character string. Filename 3'UTR analysis result, gff format. implemented.

title: Annotate TEs with Genomic Regions and Generate Region Distribution Plots — annotate_TE_regions
text: list four updated elements downstream analysis: res.TEs Data frame DESeq2 results added genomic annotation columns (annotation, geneChr, geneStart, geneEnd, geneId, gene_name, etc.) TE.count Data frame normalized TE counts added genomic annotation columns gene.count Data frame normalized gene counts metadata Original sample metadata (unchanged)

title: Annotate TEs with Genomic Regions and Generate Region Distribution Plots — annotate_TE_regions
text: function performs following steps: Imports filters gene GTF protein-coding genes Creates TxDb object ChIPseeker annotation Extracts transcript features nearest gene assignment Annotates TEs genomic context using TE_genomic_context Saves annotated results TSV file Generates region distribution plots using graphTEregion protein-coding genes considered annotation focus biologically relevant gene-TE associations. Non-coding genes pseudogenes excluded.

title: Annotate TEs with Genomic Regions and Generate Region Distribution Plots — annotate_TE_regions
text: function creates subdirectory structure:
code: output_folder/   └── TEs_annotated/       ├── DESeq2_TE_results_annotated.tsv       └── [region distribution plots in specified format(s)]

title: Annotate TEs with Genomic Regions and Generate Region Distribution Plots — annotate_TE_regions
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Run differential expression analysis first TE_results <- TE_DEA(   metafile = "metadata.txt",   folder = "counts",   gtf.TE.file = "TEs.gtf" )  # Annotate TEs and generate region plots TE_results_annotated <- annotate_TE_regions(   TE_results = TE_results,   gtf.genes.file = "genes.gtf",   output_folder = "results",   device = c("jpeg", "png"),   plot.title = "TE Distribution by Genomic Region",   minCounts = 10 )  } # }

what: Perform Differential Expression Analysis Using DESeq2 — call_deseq2
title: Perform Differential Expression Analysis Using DESeq2 — call_deseq2
text: Performs differential expression analysis count data using DESeq2. Optionally uses gene features (non-TE) size factor estimation.

title: Perform Differential Expression Analysis Using DESeq2 — call_deseq2
code: call_deseq2(countData, metadata, useCtrlGenes)

title: Perform Differential Expression Analysis Using DESeq2 — call_deseq2
text: countData Data frame count matrix returned readTEcounts. Features rows, samples columns. Row names identify features; TE features expected contain ":" names. metadata Data frame sample metadata returned read_metadata. Must contain columns: File, Sample, Group, Condition. Sample names must match column names countData. useCtrlGenes Logical. TRUE, gene features (without ":" names) used estimate size factors. FALSE, features used. Default FALSE.

title: Perform Differential Expression Analysis Using DESeq2 — call_deseq2
text: DESeqDataSet object running DESeq2 analysis pipeline.

title: Perform Differential Expression Analysis Using DESeq2 — call_deseq2
text: function creates DESeqDataSet design formula ~ condition, condition derived Condition column metadata (Control vs Treat). useCtrlGenes = TRUE, size factors estimated using gene features (rownames without ":"), can appropriate analyzing datasets containing genes transposable elements. approach assumes TEs may variable expression bias normalization. function assumes TE features contain ":" rownames (e.g., "transcript_id:TE_name:TE_family:TE_class").

title: Perform Differential Expression Analysis Using DESeq2 — call_deseq2
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Read data metadata <- read_metadata("metadata.txt") countData <- readTEcounts(metadata, "counts")  # Run DESeq2 using all features for normalization dds <- call_deseq2(countData, metadata, useCtrlGenes = FALSE)  # Run DESeq2 using only genes for normalization dds_ctrl <- call_deseq2(countData, metadata, useCtrlGenes = TRUE)  # Extract results res <- results_deseq2(dds) } # }

what: Classify TE Transcription as Self-Expressed or Gene-Dependent — classify_TE_transcription
title: Classify TE Transcription as Self-Expressed or Gene-Dependent — classify_TE_transcription
text: Classifies transposable elements based transcriptional origin: TEs transcribed promoters (self-expressed) vs. TEs transcribed part host gene transcription (gene-dependent/runthrough). Classification follows methodology Chang et al. (2021) updated Gázquez-Gutiérrez et al (2026).

title: Classify TE Transcription as Self-Expressed or Gene-Dependent — classify_TE_transcription
code: classify_TE_transcription(   TE_results,   output_folder = ".",   minNormCounts = 10,   maxpadj = 0.05,   minlfc = 1,   TE_list = c("LTR", "LINE", "DNA", "SINE"),   antisense = FALSE,   antisense_suffix = "_AS",   save = "all",   plot.title = NULL,   device = "png",   width.pie = 14,   height.pie = 7,   width.bar = 7,   height.bar = 7 )

title: Classify TE Transcription as Self-Expressed or Gene-Dependent — classify_TE_transcription
text: TE_results List object returned annotate_TE_regions, containing: res.TEs Data frame DESeq2 results TEs genomic annotations TE.count Data frame normalized counts TEs gene.count Data frame normalized counts genes metadata Sample metadata output_folder Character string. Directory saving results. subdirectory "classify_TE" created. Default current directory. minNormCounts Numeric. Minimum normalized count threshold considering feature "expressed". feature must normalized counts >= value least one condition considered expressed. Default 10. maxpadj Numeric. Maximum adjusted p-value filtering significantly dysregulated TEs save != "". Default 0.05. minlfc Numeric. Minimum absolute log2 fold change filtering significantly dysregulated TEs save != "". Default 1. TE_list Character vector. TE classes include classification. Default c("LTR", "LINE", "DNA", "SINE"). antisense Logical. TRUE, classifies TEs antisense orientation gene-dependent. Requires antisense GTF generated gtfBothStrands. Default FALSE. antisense_suffix Character string. Suffix used identify antisense features TE_element names. Default "_AS". save Character string. TEs save output file: "": expressed TEs (default) "dys": significantly dysregulated TEs () "": significantly upregulated TEs "": significantly downregulated TEs plot.title Character string. Title classification pie charts. NULL, title displayed. Default NULL. device Character vector. File format(s) output plots. Default "png". width.pie Numeric. Plot width inches. Default 14. height.pie Numeric. Plot height inches. Default 7. width.bar Numeric. Plot width inches. Default 7. height.bar Numeric. Plot height inches. Default 7. TEs classified two broad categories: Gene-dependent (runthrough transcription): Exon: TE overlaps exon, 5'UTR, 3'UTR strand Intron (expressed): TE intron expressed gene, strand Antisense: TE opposite strand (antisense = TRUE) Self-expressed (autonomous transcription): Intron (expressed): TE intron non-expressed gene Non read-: cases (promoters, intergenic, etc.) Classification Hierarchy: TE fit multiple categories, restrictive chosen: Exon > Intron (expressed) > Antisense > Intron (expressed) > Non read-Expression Determination: gene/TE considered "expressed" normalized counts ≥ minNormCounts least one condition (Control Treat).

title: Classify TE Transcription as Self-Expressed or Gene-Dependent — classify_TE_transcription
text: TE_results list updated components

title: Classify TE Transcription as Self-Expressed or Gene-Dependent — classify_TE_transcription
code: output_folder/   └── classify_TE/       ├── DESeq2_TE_results_classified_<save>.tsv       └── pie_TE_classes.<device>

title: Classify TE Transcription as Self-Expressed or Gene-Dependent — classify_TE_transcription
text: Chang NC, Rovira Q, Wells J, Feschotte C, Vaquerizas JM. (2022) Zebrafish transposable elements show extensive diversification age, genomic distribution, developmental expression. Genome Res. 2022 Jul;32(7):1408-1423. doi:10.1101/gr.275655.121  Gázquez-Gutiérrez, ., et al. (2026) Control retrotransposon-driven activation interferon response double-stranded RNA binding protein DGCR8. Nucleic Acid Research (press).

title: Classify TE Transcription as Self-Expressed or Gene-Dependent — classify_TE_transcription
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Run TE DEA first TE_results <- TE_DEA(   metafile = "metadata.txt",   folder = "counts",   gtf.TE.file = "TEs.gtf" )  # Annotate TEs and generate region plots TE_results_annotated <- annotate_TE_regions(   TE_results = TE_results,   gtf.genes.file = "genes.gtf",   output_folder = "results",   device = c("jpeg", "png"),   plot.title = "TE Distribution by Genomic Region",   minCounts = 10 )  # Classify TE transcription TE_results <- classify_TE_transcription(   TE_results = TE_results_annotated,   output_folder = "results",   minNormCounts = 10,   save = "all",   plot.title = "TE Classification" ) } # }

what: Create Donut Pie Chart for Single TE Type — create_pie_donut
title: Create Donut Pie Chart for Single TE Type — create_pie_donut
text: Creates donut/pie chart specific TE type showing breakdown expression types. Requires webr package.

title: Create Donut Pie Chart for Single TE Type — create_pie_donut
code: create_pie_donut(   res,   TE_feature,   plot.title = NULL,   output_folder = ".",   height = 7,   width = 7,   device = "png",   prefix = NULL )

title: Create Donut Pie Chart for Single TE Type — create_pie_donut
text: res Data frame classification results TE_feature Character string. Specific TE plot (e.g., "LINE", "L1") plot.title Character string. Plot title output_folder Character string. Output directory height Numeric. Plot height inches. Default 7. width Numeric. Plot width inches. Default 7. device Character vector. Output format(s). Default "png". prefix Character string. Optional prefix filename

title: Create Donut Pie Chart for Single TE Type — create_pie_donut
text: Invisible NULL. Called side effects (creating plot file).

title: Create Donut Pie Chart for Single TE Type — create_pie_donut
text: Please, note webr package maintained function throws several warnings deprecated uses.

title: Create Donut Pie Chart for Single TE Type — create_pie_donut

what: Download Test Data for Package Examples and Testing — downloadTestData
title: Download Test Data for Package Examples and Testing — downloadTestData
text: Downloads sample data files Dropbox testing running package examples. data includes human genome annotations (chromosome 1 ) TE count data, suitable demonstrating differential expression analysis workflows.

title: Download Test Data for Package Examples and Testing — downloadTestData
code: downloadTestData(folder = NULL, timeout = 300)

title: Download Test Data for Package Examples and Testing — downloadTestData
text: folder Character string. Directory test data downloaded. NULL (default), uses temporary directory via tempdir(). directory created exist. timeout Numeric. Download timeout seconds. Default 300 (5 minutes). Increase downloads fail due slow connections.

title: Download Test Data for Package Examples and Testing — downloadTestData
text: list four elements: folder Character string path download directory metafile Full path metadata CSV file (data.csv) gtf.gene.file Full path gene GTF file gtf.TE.file Full path TE GTF file

title: Download Test Data for Package Examples and Testing — downloadTestData
text: function downloads three datasets: Gene GTF (~78 MB): Human gene annotations chromosome 1 Ensembl GRCh38.115 TE GTF (~10 MB): Transposable element annotations chromosome 1 RepeatMasker (hg38) Count Data (~2 MB compressed): Example TEtranscripts count files ZIP archive. Contains 6 sample count files (3 control, 3 treatment) csv file TExpress analysis Files downloaded Dropbox using direct download links. count data ZIP file automatically extracted archive removed extraction.

title: Download Test Data for Package Examples and Testing — downloadTestData
text: following files created specified folder: Homo_sapiens.GRCh38.115.chr22.gtf - Gene annotations hg38_rmsk_TE_chr22.gtf - TE annotations *.cntTable - Multiple count files (extracted ZIP) data.csv - csv file including info count files

title: Download Test Data for Package Examples and Testing — downloadTestData
text: Total download size approximately 90 MB. Ensure adequate disk space internet bandwidth. slow connections, increase timeout parameter.

title: Download Test Data for Package Examples and Testing — downloadTestData
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Download to temporary directory (default) my.data <- downloadTestData()  # Download to specific directory my.data <- downloadTestData(folder = "test_data")  # Use downloaded data in analysis results <- TE_DEA(   metafile = my.data$metafile,   folder = my.data$folder,   gtf.TE.file = my.data$gtf.TE.file ) } # }

what: Plot Differential Expression Analysis Results for Transposable Elements — graphTE_DEA
title: Plot Differential Expression Analysis Results for Transposable Elements — graphTE_DEA
text: Creates volcano MA plots DESeq2 differential expression results saves multiple file formats.

title: Plot Differential Expression Analysis Results for Transposable Elements — graphTE_DEA
code: graphTE_DEA(   res,   maxpadj = 0.05,   minlfc = 1,   device = "png",   output_folder = ".",   width = 7,   height = 7,   plot.title = NULL )

title: Plot Differential Expression Analysis Results for Transposable Elements — graphTE_DEA
text: res Data frame DESeqResults object containing differential expression results. Must include columns: log2FoldChange, padj, baseMean. maxpadj Numeric. Adjusted p-value threshold significance. Features padj < maxpadj considered significant. Default 0.05. minlfc Numeric. Minimum absolute log2 fold change threshold differential expression. Features |log2FC| > minlfc considered differentially expressed. Default 1. device Character vector. Output file format(s). Supported formats: "svg", "eps", "png", "tiff", "jpeg". Multiple formats can specified vector (e.g., c("tiff", "png")). Default "png". output_folder Character string. Directory plots saved. Must exist creatable. Default current directory ("."). width Numeric. Plot width inches. Default 7. height Numeric. Plot height inches. Default 7. plot.title Character string. Title plots. NULL (default), uses generic titles "Volcano Plot" "MA Plot".

title: Plot Differential Expression Analysis Results for Transposable Elements — graphTE_DEA
text: return object

title: Plot Differential Expression Analysis Results for Transposable Elements — graphTE_DEA
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Assume 'results' is a DESeq2 results object library(DESeq2)  # Basic usage - save as PNG in current directory graphTE_DEA(   res = results,   maxpadj = 0.05,   minlfc = 1 )  # Save in multiple formats with custom title graphTE_DEA(   res = results,   maxpadj = 0.01,   minlfc = 1.5,   device = c("png", "svg"),   output_folder = "results/plots",   width = 10,   height = 8,   plot.title = "KO vs WT" )  # Custom dimensions for publication graphTE_DEA(   res = results,   maxpadj = 0.05,   minlfc = 1,   device = "jpeg",   output_folder = "figures",   width = 6,   height = 5,   plot.title = "Differential TE Expression" ) } # }

what: Generate Genomic Region and TE Class Distribution Plots — graphTEregion
title: Generate Genomic Region and TE Class Distribution Plots — graphTEregion
text: Creates stacked bar plots showing distribution transposable elements across genomic regions (promoters, exons, introns, etc.) TE classes (LINE, SINE, LTR, DNA) different sample groups differential expression categories.

title: Generate Genomic Region and TE Class Distribution Plots — graphTEregion
code: graphTEregion(   TE_results,   device = "png",   output_folder = ".",   width = 10,   height = 7,   minCounts = 10,   minlfc = 1,   maxpadj = 0.05,   plot.title = NULL )

title: Generate Genomic Region and TE Class Distribution Plots — graphTEregion
text: TE_results List object returned TE_DEA annotated annotate_TE_regions, containing: res.TEs Data frame DESeq2 results genomic annotations. Must include columns: log2FoldChange, padj, annotation, TE_class TE.count Data frame normalized counts genomic annotations. Columns sample names plus annotation columns gene.count Data frame normalized counts genes metadata Sample metadata columns: Sample, Condition device Character vector. File format(s) output plots. Supported: "svg", "eps", "png", "tiff", "jpeg". Can specify multiple formats. Default "png". output_folder Character string. Directory plots saved. Default current directory ("."). width Numeric. Plot width inches. Default 10. height Numeric. Plot height inches. Default 7. minCounts Numeric. Minimum total normalized counts threshold considering TE "expressed" condition. TEs threshold across samples condition excluded. Default 10. minlfc Numeric. Minimum absolute log2 fold change defining /-regulated TEs. Default 1. maxpadj Numeric. Maximum adjusted p-value defining significant differential expression. Default 0.05. plot.title Character string. Title plots. NULL, title added. Default NULL.

title: Generate Genomic Region and TE Class Distribution Plots — graphTEregion
text: Invisible NULL. Called side effects (creating plot files).

title: Generate Genomic Region and TE Class Distribution Plots — graphTEregion
text: function generates two types stacked bar plots: Genomic Region Distribution: Shows percentage TEs genomic context (Promoter, 5' UTR, Exon, Intron, 3' UTR, Downstream, Intergenic) TE Class Distribution: Shows percentage different TE classes (LINE, SINE, LTR, DNA, ) Four categories plotted : Control: TEs expressed (total counts > minCounts) control samples Treatment: TEs expressed treatment samples : Significantly -regulated TEs (log2FC < -minlfc, padj < maxpadj) : Significantly -regulated TEs (log2FC > minlfc, padj < maxpadj) Sample group names derived common prefix sample names condition (e.g., "WT1, WT2, WT3" becomes "WT").

title: Generate Genomic Region and TE Class Distribution Plots — graphTEregion
text: Two plot files created specified format(s): stackBar_region.<format>: Genomic region distribution stackBar_TEclass.<format>: TE class distribution

title: Generate Genomic Region and TE Class Distribution Plots — graphTEregion
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # After running TE_DEA and TE_regionAnnot TE_results <- TE_DEA(   metafile = "metadata.txt",   folder = "counts",   gtf.TE.file = "TEs.gtf" )  TE_results <- TE_regionAnnot(   TE_results = TE_results,   gtf.genes.file = "genes.gtf" )  # Generate region distribution plots graphTEregion(   TE_results = TE_results,   device = c("tiff", "png"),   output_folder = "results/plots",   width = 10,   height = 7,   minCounts = 10,   minlfc = 1,   maxpadj = 0.05,   plot.title = "TE Distribution" ) } # }

what: Create Classification Plots for TE Expression — graph_classify_TE
title: Create Classification Plots for TE Expression — graph_classify_TE
text: Generates multiple types plots showing proportion self-expressed vs. gene-dependent transposable elements across different TE classes (LINE, SINE, LTR, DNA, etc.).

title: Create Classification Plots for TE Expression — graph_classify_TE
code: graph_classify_TE(   res,   plot.title = NULL,   device = "png",   width.pie = 14,   height.pie = 7,   width.bar = 7,   height.bar = 7,   output_folder = ".",   colors = c(dependent = "#FF1F5B", self = "#009ADE"),   labels = c(dependent = "Gene-dependent TEs", self = "Self-expressed TEs"),   save = "all" )

title: Create Classification Plots for TE Expression — graph_classify_TE
text: res Data frame containing TE classification results. Must include columns: TE_expression (classification: "dependent" "self"), expression_type (detailed classification stacked bar plots). plot.title Character string. Title plot. NULL (default), title displayed. device Character vector. File format(s) output plots. Supported: "pdf", "svg", "eps", "png", "tiff", "jpeg". Default "png". width.pie Numeric. Plot width inches pie graph. Default 14 (suitable 4-6 TE classes). height.pie Numeric. Plot height inches pie graph. Default 7. width.bar Numeric. Plot width inches stack. Default 7 (suitable 4-6 TE classes). height.bar Numeric. Plot height inches stack bar. Default 7. output_folder Character string. Directory plots saved. Default current directory ("."). colors Named character vector. Colors TE expression types. Default c("dependent" = "#FF1F5B", "self" = "#009ADE"). Names must match values TE_expression column. labels Named character vector. Labels TE expression types legend. Default c("dependent" = "Gene-dependent TEs", "self" = "Self-expressed TEs"). save Character string. TEs save output file: "": expressed TEs (default) "dys": significantly dysregulated TEs () "": significantly upregulated TEs "": significantly downregulated TEs function creates three types plots: 1. Pie Charts: One pie chart per TE class showing overall proportion : Self-expressed TEs: Transcribed promoter Gene-dependent TEs: Transcribed part gene runthrough 2. Stacked Bar Charts: Grouped bars showing detailed classification breakdown: Exon, Intron (expressed), Intron (expressed), etc. Grouped self-expressed vs. gene-dependent One panel per TE class 3. Bar plot: One bar per TE class showing overall proportion absolute number TE elements en class.

title: Create Classification Plots for TE Expression — graph_classify_TE
text: Invisible NULL. Called side effects (creating plot files).

title: Create Classification Plots for TE Expression — graph_classify_TE
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Assuming 'classified_TEs' has TE_expression and TE_class columns graph_classify_TE(   res = TE_results$res.TEs,   plot.title = "TE Expression Classification",   device = c("pdf", "png"),   output_folder = "results/classification" ) } # }

what: Create Antisense TE GTF with Both Strands — gtfBothStrands
title: Create Antisense TE GTF with Both Strands — gtfBothStrands
text: Generates GTF file containing sense antisense annotations duplicating features inverting strand antisense copies. Useful analyzing antisense transcription bidirectional promoters.

title: Create Antisense TE GTF with Both Strands — gtfBothStrands
code: gtfBothStrands(gtf.file, output.file = NULL, add_suffix = "_AS")

title: Create Antisense TE GTF with Both Strands — gtfBothStrands
text: gtf.file Character string. Path input TE GTF file. file read, duplicated strand inversion, exported new file "_AS" suffix. output.file Character string. Optional path output TE GTF file. NULL (default), creates file directory input "_AS" added extension (e.g., "hg38_rmsk_TE.gtf" becomes "hg38_rmsk_TE_AS.gtf"). add_suffix Character string. Suffix add gene_id transcript_id antisense features. Default "_AS".

title: Create Antisense TE GTF with Both Strands — gtfBothStrands
text: Character string (invisibly). Path created output GTF file. Also prints message file location feature counts.

title: Create Antisense TE GTF with Both Strands — gtfBothStrands
text: function performs following steps: Imports input TE GTF file Creates antisense copies inverting strand information Appends suffix gene_id transcript_id antisense copies Merges sense antisense features Sorts genomic coordinates Exports new TE GTF file feature "+" strand, antisense copy "-" strand created, vice versa. Gene transcript IDs modified distinguish antisense features (e.g., "ENSG00000000001" becomes "ENSG00000000001_AS").

title: Create Antisense TE GTF with Both Strands — gtfBothStrands
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create antisense GTF with default naming gtfBothStrands("genes.gtf") # Output: genes_AS.gtf  # Specify custom output file gtfBothStrands(   gtf.file = "hg38_rmsk_TE.gtf",   output.file = "hg38_rmsk_TE_AS.gtf" )  # Custom suffix for antisense features gtfBothStrands(   gtf.file = "hg38_rmsk_TE.gtf",   add_suffix = "_antisense" ) } # }

what: Import and Standardize GTF/GFF Annotation File — importGTF
title: Import and Standardize GTF/GFF Annotation File — importGTF
text: Imports GTF GFF annotation file standardizes keeping standard chromosomes converting UCSC chromosome naming style (e.g., "chr1" instead "1").

title: Import and Standardize GTF/GFF Annotation File — importGTF
code: importGTF(   gtf.file,   format = "gtf",   keep.standard.chroms = TRUE,   seqlevels.style = "UCSC" )

title: Import and Standardize GTF/GFF Annotation File — importGTF
text: gtf.file Character string. Path GTF GFF format annotation file. Can gzip-compressed (.gz). format Character string. Format annotation file. Must either "gtf" "gff" (also accepts "gff3"). Default "gtf". keep.standard.chroms Logical. TRUE (default), removes non-standard chromosomes (keeps autosomes, sex chromosomes, mitochondria). Scaffolds, alternative haplotypes, patches removed. seqlevels.style Character string. Chromosome naming style use. Options include "UCSC" (chr1, chr2, ...), "NCBI" (1, 2, ...), "Ensembl" (1, 2, ...). Default "UCSC". Use NULL skip style conversion.

title: Import and Standardize GTF/GFF Annotation File — importGTF
text: GRanges object containing genomic annotations standardized chromosome names filtered standard chromosomes.

title: Import and Standardize GTF/GFF Annotation File — importGTF
text: function performs three operations: Imports GTF/GFF file using rtracklayer::import Optionally filters standard chromosomes (autosomes, sex chromosomes, mitochondria) using GenomeInfoDb::keepStandardChromosomes Optionally converts chromosome naming specified style using GenomeInfoDb::seqlevelsStyle

title: Import and Standardize GTF/GFF Annotation File — importGTF
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Import GTF file with default settings gtf <- importGTF("annotations.gtf")  # Import GFF3 file gff <- importGTF("annotations.gff3", format = "gff3")  # Import without filtering chromosomes gtf_all <- importGTF("annotations.gtf", keep.standard.chroms = FALSE)  # Import and keep NCBI chromosome style (1, 2, 3...) gtf_ncbi <- importGTF("annotations.gtf", seqlevels.style = "NCBI")  # Import for a specific species gtf_mouse <- importGTF(   "mouse_annotations.gtf",   species = "Mus_musculus" )  # Import compressed file gtf <- importGTF("annotations.gtf.gz") } # }

what: Get Normalized Counts from DESeq2 Object — norm_counts
title: Get Normalized Counts from DESeq2 Object — norm_counts
text: Extracts normalized count data DESeqDataSet object. Counts normalized size factors account differences sequencing depth samples.

title: Get Normalized Counts from DESeq2 Object — norm_counts
code: norm_counts(dds)

title: Get Normalized Counts from DESeq2 Object — norm_counts
text: dds DESeqDataSet object returned call_deseq2. Size factors must calculated (automatically done DESeq2::DESeq()).

title: Get Normalized Counts from DESeq2 Object — norm_counts
text: numeric matrix normalized counts. Features rows, samples columns. Row column names preserved DESeqDataSet.

title: Get Normalized Counts from DESeq2 Object — norm_counts
text: convenience wrapper around DESeq2::counts(dds, normalized = TRUE). Normalized counts calculated dividing raw counts size factors. counts appropriate visualization clustering, used input differential expression analysis (use raw counts ).

title: Get Normalized Counts from DESeq2 Object — norm_counts
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Run DESeq2 analysis dds <- call_deseq2(countData, metadata)  # Extract normalized counts norm_counts_matrix <- norm_counts(dds)  # Convert to data frame if needed norm_counts_df <- as.data.frame(norm_counts_matrix) } # }

what: Save ggplot to Multiple File Formats — printDevice
title: Save ggplot to Multiple File Formats — printDevice
text: Internal function save ggplot object one file formats. Supports common vector (SVG, EPS) raster (PNG, TIFF, JPEG) formats.

title: Save ggplot to Multiple File Formats — printDevice
code: printDevice(plot, basename, device, width = 7, height = 7)

title: Save ggplot to Multiple File Formats — printDevice
text: plot ggplot object save. basename Character string. Base name output files (without extension). Extensions added automatically based requested formats. device Character vector. File format(s) save. Supported formats: "svg", "eps", "png", "tiff", "jpeg". Case-insensitive. Dots automatically removed (e.g., ".png" becomes "png"). width Numeric. Plot width inches. Default 7. height Numeric. Plot height inches. Default 7.

what: Read and Merge TE Count Data by Sample — readTEcounts
title: Read and Merge TE Count Data by Sample — readTEcounts
text: Reads transposable element (TE) count files multiple samples merges single count matrix. Count files TElocal (TEtranscripts) tab-separated two columns: feature names counts.

title: Read and Merge TE Count Data by Sample — readTEcounts
code: readTEcounts(metadata, folder)

title: Read and Merge TE Count Data by Sample — readTEcounts
text: metadata Data frame sample metadata, returned read_metadata. Must contain columns: File, Sample, Group, Condition. folder Character string. Path directory containing count files listed first column metadata. Can absolute relative path.

title: Read and Merge TE Count Data by Sample — readTEcounts
text: data frame features row names samples columns. Column names correspond Sample column metadata. Column order matches row order metadata.

title: Read and Merge TE Count Data by Sample — readTEcounts
text: count file must: tab-separated header row Contain exactly 2 columns: feature name count value Use feature names across samples (order can vary) function attempts efficiently merge count data: files identical features order, uses fast column binding feature order differs, performs full outer join align features Missing features samples filled NA (occur well-formatted data)

title: Read and Merge TE Count Data by Sample — readTEcounts
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Read metadata metadata <- read_metadata("metadata.txt")  # Read count files from directory countData <- readTEcounts(metadata, folder = "counts")  # Verify dimensions dim(countData) head(countData) } # }

what: Read Metadata File with Sample Information — read_metadata
title: Read Metadata File with Sample Information — read_metadata
text: Reads validates tab-separated metadata file containing sample information differential expression analysis. file must contain specific columns follow formatting requirements DESeq2 compatibility.

title: Read Metadata File with Sample Information — read_metadata
code: read_metadata(datafile)

title: Read Metadata File with Sample Information — read_metadata
text: datafile Character string. Path tab-separated file (.txt, .tsv, .csv) containing metadata. Must exactly 4 columns headers: File, Sample, Group, Condition.

title: Read Metadata File with Sample Information — read_metadata
text: data frame four columns (File, Sample, Group, Condition) validated metadata. Condition column converted factor "Control" reference level.

title: Read Metadata File with Sample Information — read_metadata
text: metadata file must follow requirements: Exactly 4 columns headers: File, Sample, Group, Condition least one row data (beyond header) "Condition" column must contain exactly two unique values: "Control" "Treat" duplicate values "Sample" "File" columns Tab-separated format (delimiters supported) "Control" "Treat" labels Condition column required proper DESeq2 analysis, "Control" set reference level.

title: Read Metadata File with Sample Information — read_metadata
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Read metadata from file metadata <- read_metadata("path/to/metadata.txt")  # Example valid file structure: # File            Sample  Group   Condition # WT1.cntTable    WT1     WT      Control # WT2.cntTable    WT2     WT      Control # KO1.cntTable    KO1     KO      Treat # KO2.cntTable    KO2     KO      Treat } # }

what: Get Results After DESeq2 Analysis — results_deseq2
title: Get Results After DESeq2 Analysis — results_deseq2
text: Extracts differential expression results DESeqDataSet object, optional log2 fold change shrinkage improved effect size estimates.

title: Get Results After DESeq2 Analysis — results_deseq2
code: results_deseq2(dds, shrinklog2FC = FALSE)

title: Get Results After DESeq2 Analysis — results_deseq2
text: dds DESeqDataSet object returned call_deseq2 DESeq2::DESeq(). Must completed DESeq2 analysis pipeline. shrinklog2FC Logical. TRUE, applies log2 fold change shrinkage using apeglm method produce accurate effect size estimates. FALSE, returns standard DESeq2 results. Default FALSE. Note: Shrinkage requires apeglm package installed.

title: Get Results After DESeq2 Analysis — results_deseq2
text: data frame DESeq2 results rows sorted adjusted p-value (significant first). Row names correspond feature names count data.

title: Get Results After DESeq2 Analysis — results_deseq2
text: function extracts differential expression results comparing "Treat" condition "Control" condition (reference level). shrinklog2FC = TRUE, log2 fold changes shrunk using apeglm method (DESeq2::lfcShrink) Rows NA values (typically low-count genes filtered DESeq2's independent filtering) removed, results sorted adjusted p-value.

title: Get Results After DESeq2 Analysis — results_deseq2
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Run DESeq2 analysis dds <- call_deseq2(countData, metadata, useCtrlGenes = FALSE)  # Get results without shrinkage res <- results_deseq2(dds, shrinklog2FC = FALSE)  # Get results with log2FC shrinkage (requires apeglm package) res_shrink <- results_deseq2(dds, shrinklog2FC = TRUE)  # View top results head(res) } # }

what: Create violin plot for specific TE list — violinPlotByTEList
title: Create violin plot for specific TE list — violinPlotByTEList
text: Generates violin plot showing log2 fold changes specified list transposable elements (TEs).

title: Create violin plot for specific TE list — violinPlotByTEList
code: violinPlotByTEList(   res.TEs,   TE_list,   broad_type = NULL,   minlfc = 1,   maxpadj = 0.05,   min.N = 10,   width = 7,   height = 7,   device = "png",   output_folder = ".",   plot.title = "Violin plot" )

title: Create violin plot for specific TE list — violinPlotByTEList
text: res.TEs Data frame containing TE differential expression results columns: log2FoldChange, padj, TE_element, TE_name, TE_family, TE_class TE_list Character vector TE identifiers plot. elements belong hierarchical level broad_type Character string specifying TE classification level (e.g., "TE_class", "TE_family"). NULL, automatically detected TE_list minlfc Numeric minimum log2 fold change threshold significance (default: 1) maxpadj Numeric maximum adjusted p-value threshold significance (default: 0.05) min.N Integer minimum number elements required per group (default: 10) width Numeric plot width inches (default: 7) height Numeric plot height inches (default: 7) device Character string specifying output device (default: "png") output_folder Character string path output directory (default: ".") plot.title Character string plot title (default: "Violin plot")

title: Create violin plot for specific TE list — violinPlotByTEList
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Saves plot file side effect.

title: Create violin plot for specific TE list — violinPlotByTEList
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ violinPlotByTEList(res.TEs, TE_list = c("LINE", "SINE", "LTR", "DNA"),                    minlfc = 1.5, maxpadj = 0.01)  violinPlotByTEList(res.TEs, TE_list = c("Alu", "L1", "ERV1", "SVA"),                    minlfc = 1.5, maxpadj = 0.01) } # }

what: Create violin plot for specific TE type — violinPlotByTEtype
title: Create violin plot for specific TE type — violinPlotByTEtype
text: Generates violin plot showing dysregulated transposable elements within specific TE type, organized finer classification level.

title: Create violin plot for specific TE type — violinPlotByTEtype
code: violinPlotByTEtype(   res.TEs,   TE_type,   specific_type = "TE_name",   broad_type = NULL,   order = "up",   minlfc = 1,   maxpadj = 0.05,   nTop = 6,   min.N = 10,   width = 7,   height = 7,   device = "png",   output_folder = ".",   plot.title = "Violin plot" )

title: Create violin plot for specific TE type — violinPlotByTEtype
text: res.TEs Data frame containing TE differential expression results columns: log2FoldChange, padj, TE_element, TE_name, TE_family, TE_class TE_type Character string specifying TE type analyze (example: "LTR", "Alu") specific_type Character string finer classification level: "TE_family" "TE_name" (default: "TE_name") broad_type Character string broader classification level: "TE_class" "TE_family". NULL, automatically detected TE_type (default: NULL) order Character string specifying expression direction: "", "", "" (default: "") minlfc Numeric minimum log2 fold change threshold significance (default: 1) maxpadj Numeric maximum adjusted p-value threshold significance (default: 0.05) nTop Integer number top dysregulated elements display (default: 6) min.N Integer minimum number elements required per group (default: 10) width Numeric plot width inches (default: 7) height Numeric plot height inches (default: 7) device Character string specifying output device (default: "png") output_folder Character string path output directory (default: ".") plot.title Character string plot title (default: "Violin plot")

title: Create violin plot for specific TE type — violinPlotByTEtype
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Saves plot file side effect.

title: Create violin plot for specific TE type — violinPlotByTEtype
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ violinPlotByTEtype(res.TEs, TE_type = "LINE",                    specific_type = "TE_family", nTop = 10)   violinPlotByTEtype(res.TEs, TE_type = "LINE",                    specific_type = "TE_name", nTop = 10) } # }

title: TExpress 1.0
text: First public release. Differential expression analysis transposable elements TElocal count tables via TE_DEA() (DESeq2-based, Treat-vs-Control contrast). Genomic-context annotation TEs relative gene GTF annotate_TE_regions() (Promoter / 5’ UTR / Exon / Intron / 3’ UTR / Downstream / Intergenic). Transcriptional classification TEs self-expressed gene-dependent classify_TE_transcription(). GTF utilities: filter_GTF(), filter_TE_annot(), gtfBothStrands(). Publication-ready plots: volcano/MA plots, violin plots, stacked bar plots genomic-region TE-class distributions. downloadTestData() fetch small chr22 example dataset testing package examples.
